our_argument_summary: Our review says MHD simulations (citing Terra-Nova et al. 2017) reproduce SAA splitting from reversed-flux patches at the CMB. The dome model has no MHD equations, no core dynamics, no mechanism for reversed-flux patches. The two SAA cells drift westward together at ~0.2-0.3°/year while separating at ~0.8°/year — signature of flow-driven advection in a rotating spherical shell. MHD explains the African cell's faster decay via the African large-low-shear-velocity province. V51.0 concedes the 'station ratio proxy method' was 'methodologically invalid.'

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt — WIN-004 entry and PROS-001 prospective claim
raw-text/03-predictions.txt — PRED-013 long-term prediction (120-180 deg by 2055)
No monitor.py or inject_ai_layer.py available in raw-text/ to verify monitoring code claims
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Cannot verify directly — monitor.py is not present in raw-text/. Cycle 1 review claims domain 8 (SAA Decay Rate at Tsumeb TTB) references exponential decay monitoring, and inject_ai_layer.py hardcodes 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg'. These claims from Cycle 1 cannot be re-verified without the source files.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Cannot verify directly. Cycle 1 review states the 50.57 deg value is hardcoded in inject_ai_layer.py (not a time-dependent function). Current code_analysis tag says 'live_fetch' — this CONTRADICTS Cycle 1 review which describes the monitoring as focused on SAA decay rate (>=28 nT/year), not cell separation. The 'live_fetch' tag may be referring to the decay rate monitoring rather than separation monitoring.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model (Finlay et al. 2020) — dome claims 2000-2025 data but CHAOS-7 official coverage ends ~2020
NOAA WMM data — possible extension for 2021-2025
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: If separation slows: dome can invoke 'aetheric damping threshold.' If separation accelerates: dome claims vindication. The core claim 'globe has no mechanism' is a negative assertion easily dismissed by dome supporters when presented with counterevidence.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: CRITICAL: The code_analysis tag 'monitoring: live_fetch' may be incorrect. Cycle 1 review describes monitor.py domain 8 as monitoring SAA DECAY RATE (>=28 nT/year at Tsumeb), not cell SEPARATION rate. These are different quantities. If monitor.py tracks decay rate but not separation, the 'live_fetch' tag describes the wrong WIN. Cannot verify without monitor.py source. Additionally, inject_ai_layer.py reportedly hardcodes '50.57 deg' for separation — if so, separation monitoring is hardcoded, not live_fetch.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Retaining 'live_fetch' from Cycle 1 with CAVEAT: Cycle 1 review says domain 8 monitors SAA decay rate at Tsumeb (TTB), which is adjacent but not identical to cell separation. Without monitor.py source code, cannot verify whether separation itself is live-fetched or only decay rate. The inject_ai_layer.py reportedly hardcodes the separation value (50.57 deg), suggesting the separation monitoring is actually hardcoded while decay rate may be live. Flagged for decider to investigate.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA splitting is well-explained by standard MHD core dynamics — reversed flux patches at the CMB generate the SAA and its bifurcation. Terra-Nova et al. (2017, PEPI) and CHAOS-7 model both explain the phenomenon. Dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric field decay' while the observed phenomenon is identical.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 data (Finlay et al. 2020) documenting SAA bifurcation predates the dome model's claim. The 30.8-50.6 deg separation (2000-2025) was observable in published paleomagnetic inversions before the dome model incorporated it. Dome's PROS-001 claims 'prospective' status but the underlying observation was already published.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula in the dome's published material derives the separation rate from dome geometric parameters (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric field equations). The dome states the separation 'follows the exponential decay curve of the aetheric vortex' but provides no equation: exp(t/tau) with tau derived from dome parameters. The prediction is qualitative ('exponential') not quantitative (no tau specified).

holes_found.description: WRONG DOI: Our evidence links to doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2017.06.036 which resolves to Fukai & Yokoyama (2017) 'Neodymium isotope heterogeneity of ordinary and carbonaceous chondrites' in EPSL — a completely unrelated geochemistry paper. The actual Terra-Nova et al. (2017) paper is 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches' published in Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors with DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002. This same wrong DOI also appears in WIN-005. A dome defender clicking this link would immediately see it goes to the wrong paper, destroying our credibility on this entire argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace DOI in both WIN-004 and WIN-005 detail_evidence: change 'https://doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2017.06.036' to 'https://doi.org/10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002'. Also change the journal context — Terra-Nova et al. was published in PEPI, not EPSL.

holes_found.description: Overstated claim about what Terra-Nova et al. (2017) demonstrates. We say it reproduces 'SAA splitting — including the timing, geometry, and rate of separation.' The paper (PEPI 2017) relates the SAA to reversed flux patches at the CMB and examines preferred locations of weak surface field. It does NOT explicitly model the temporal rate of cell bifurcation (30.8→50.6 deg over 25 years). Cycle 1 review flagged this same issue but it was not corrected.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften claim to: 'Terra-Nova et al. (2017) demonstrate that reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary determine the SAA's location and morphology, providing the physical mechanism for its spatial structure and evolution.' Remove 'including the timing, geometry, and rate of separation' which overstates the paper's scope.

holes_found.description: The 'station ratio proxy method' concession by V51.0 is cited ('V51.0 now acknowledges WIN-004's station ratio proxy method was methodologically invalid') but this phrase does not appear anywhere in our raw-text extracts of the dome's V50.6 or V51.0 content. The dome's 02-wins.txt simply says the SAA cells separated from 30.8 to 50.6 degrees — no mention of 'station ratio proxy' or methodology being invalid. Either this quote comes from a source not in our raw-text archive, or it's been misattributed.
holes_found.recommendation: Verify the source of the 'methodologically invalid' quote. If it's from the dome's git commit history or a changelog not captured in raw-text, cite the specific source. If it cannot be verified, remove the claim or soften to 'The dome model has revised its methodology for this WIN across versions.'

holes_found.description: code_analysis tag 'monitoring: live_fetch' may be inaccurate. Cycle 1 review describes domain 8 as monitoring SAA DECAY RATE at Tsumeb (>=28 nT/year), which is WIN-037/042 territory, not cell SEPARATION (WIN-004). The inject_ai_layer.py reportedly hardcodes the separation value (50.57 deg). If separation monitoring is hardcoded while only decay rate is live, the tag should be 'hardcoded' for WIN-004 specifically.
holes_found.recommendation: Flag for investigation: obtain monitor.py source and verify whether domain 8 actually monitors cell separation or only field decay rate. If the latter, change WIN-004 monitoring tag to 'hardcoded' or 'none' depending on findings.

stronger_arguments.description: DATA LINEARITY TEST: The dome claims 'exponential separation' but provides only two endpoints (30.8 deg in 2000, 50.6 deg in 2025). Linear fit: 0.792 deg/year constant rate. Exponential fit: requires tau specification which the dome never provides. With only two data points, BOTH models fit perfectly. The testable prediction: by 2035, linear model predicts ~58.5 deg; exponential (tau=55yr to hit 120 by 2055) predicts ~65 deg. This is a concrete future discriminator that our text should highlight.
sources: CHAOS-7 model data, 2000-2025 angular separation measurements

stronger_arguments.description: PRED-013 FALSIFIABILITY: The dome's own PRED-013 predicts 120-180 deg separation by 2055. At the current LINEAR rate of ~0.8 deg/year, separation by 2055 would be ~70.6 deg (from 50.6 + 0.8*25). To reach 120 deg by 2055, the rate must ACCELERATE to ~2.8 deg/year — 3.5x the current rate. This is a genuinely testable prediction that would decisively distinguish the models. Our review should highlight this as a credit to the dome model for making a falsifiable claim, while noting the required acceleration is dramatic.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies a real and scientifically significant phenomenon: the SAA has bifurcated into two distinct cells, and they are separating. This is confirmed by CHAOS-7 data. The dome also makes a genuinely testable long-term prediction (PRED-013: 120-180 deg by 2055) which most pseudoscientific models avoid. The prospective framing (PROS-001) at least attempts the distinction between prediction and retrodiction, even if the execution is questionable.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Correctly identifying a phenomenon does not validate the proposed mechanism. MHD core dynamics provide a first-principles physical mechanism (reversed flux patches, core-mantle boundary flow) while the dome's 'aetheric vortex decay' is a verbal label with no mathematical derivation. The dome claims 'globe has no mechanism' for the exponential rate — but the dome itself provides no derivation of WHY the rate should be exponential rather than linear, logarithmic, or any other functional form. Both models currently lack a precise prediction of the temporal separation rate; the dome's advantage is merely rhetorical.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-004 Cycle 2 review reveals a critical citation error that persisted through Cycle 1: the DOI links to the wrong paper entirely. This is exactly the kind of error a dome defender would use to discredit our review ('they can't even cite the right paper — how can we trust their science?'). The Cycle 1 review correctly identified the overstatement of Terra-Nova et al.'s scope (it explains SAA morphology, not specifically the bifurcation rate) but this was not fixed in the text.

The verdict 'Std Model Explains' remains correct. MHD core dynamics DO explain the SAA and its evolution, even if no published simulation has specifically predicted the temporal rate of cell separation. The dome's counter-argument that 'globe has no mechanism for the exponential rate' is misleading — the dome ALSO has no mechanism (no derivation from dome parameters). The dome's advantage is purely rhetorical: it claims an exponential rate without deriving it, then challenges the globe to match a prediction it never actually made from first principles.

The monitoring tag situation needs investigation. If monitor.py domain 8 tracks SAA decay RATE (a quantity related to WIN-037/042) rather than cell SEPARATION (WIN-004), then WIN-004's 'live_fetch' tag may be inherited from the wrong WIN. The inject_ai_layer.py hardcoded value (50.57 deg) suggests separation is hardcoded, not live-fetched.

Priority fixes: (1) Correct the DOI — this is critical and affects WIN-005 too. (2) Soften the Terra-Nova scope claim. (3) Verify the 'methodologically invalid' quote source. (4) Investigate monitoring tag accuracy.

citations_verified: CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model — DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01163-9 (Finlay et al., 2020, Earth Planets Space) — referenced in WIN-005, likely correct

Correct DOI: 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 — 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches' in Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors. This error appears in BOTH WIN-004 and WIN-005.
